# Security / Policy SSOT

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/securitydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

Executable SSOT: [`security.riido.json`](security.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `security-docs`.

This C7 entrypoint is generated from executable security wrapper SSOT and delegates detailed policy evidence to focused security tools.

The root remains a stable compatibility surface for provider full-access harness and native config overlay anchors consumed by existing CI.

## Compatibility Markers

### 4.3 Provider full-access runtime harness

Provider full-access runtime harness marker retained for downstream
conformance checks. The policy does not say provider default 가 full-access,
and it does not say caller defaults own the sandbox. Instead, default sandbox 가
danger-full-access 인 provider behavior is rejected as an implicit policy.

Codex adapter 가 danger-full-access launch
envelope 만 생성 when the daemon-owned harness explicitly selects it:

daemon 이 Codex 를 전권 host automation surface 로 실행할 때도 harness,
lease, heartbeat, and evidence stay daemon-owned. Claude / Cursor / OpenClaw 도 같은 메타 모델 아래에서 provider-specific trusted-runtime envelope 로만
확장한다.

```text
codex --sandbox danger-full-access app-server --listen stdio://
```

- No user-global native config overlay

## Executable Assertions

- C7 root docs must be generated while focused policy evidence stays with dedicated sidecar tools.
- Provider full-access harness anchors are compatibility evidence, not a provider default or caller-selected sandbox policy.
- Native config overlay remains default-deny and no user-global native config overlay is allowed by this root policy.
